title: planchonella chrysophylla
intro: Planchonella chrysophylla, also known as the Yellow Satinwood, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a tree native to New Caledonia, known for its beautiful yellow flowers and smooth, gray bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the Planchonella chrysophylla can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
